taxonomy_boundary.HoloVerify: Separate benchmark/evidence mode and not production customer mode.
taxonomy_boundary.HoloChat: Connected surface only where relevant to deployment scope.
taxonomy_boundary.HoloBuild: Connected surface only where relevant to deployment scope.
taxonomy_boundary.HoloBrain: Optional/conditional only if enabled in deployment scope.

executive_summary.summary: HoloEngine has a repo-backed trust foundation but does not yet have operating evidence sufficient for stronger compliance claims. The next 90 days should focus on assigning owners, building the trust binder, converting target-only controls into evidence, collecting provider/privacy/deployment proof, and deciding whether formal SOC 2 Type I scoping is worth funding.
executive_summary.near_term_goal: Answer enterprise diligence honestly with draft/gap-closure language while building evidence for selected controls.
executive_summary.not_the_goal: Do not claim SOC 2 readiness, certification, ISO certification, HIPAA readiness, DPA/BAA coverage, provider zero retention, data residency guarantees, production operating effectiveness, RAM-only processing, 0 ms retention, or that customer payloads are never sent to providers.

current_trust_posture.current_posture: Draft architecture exists for target production customer mode, HoloVerify benchmark/evidence mode, provider routing, metadata-only target logging, and connected surfaces.
current_trust_posture.roadmap_treatment: Use as the system-description baseline, but verify implementation before external claims.

current_trust_posture.current_posture: Draft gap assessment maps Security, Availability where applicable, Confidentiality, Privacy where applicable, and Processing Integrity where applicable.
current_trust_posture.roadmap_treatment: Convert the gap assessment into a control owner matrix and evidence tracker.

current_trust_posture.current_posture: OpenAI, xAI, and MiniMax are listed with retention, DPA, BAA/HIPAA, subprocessor, zero-retention, and residency posture marked VERIFY/TBD or TBD.
current_trust_posture.roadmap_treatment: Start contract and configuration review; do not claim coverage until reviewed.

current_trust_posture.current_posture: Safe, conditional, and blocked claim language exists.
current_trust_posture.roadmap_treatment: Use the register as the only buyer-facing wording source until Legal/Trust approve changes.

current_trust_posture.current_posture: Identity/auth, RBAC, tenant isolation, encryption evidence, secrets, logging/monitoring, incident response, SDLC, vulnerability management, deletion, support access, and backup/DR remain missing, target-only, or unverified.
current_trust_posture.roadmap_treatment: Prioritize controls by buyer frequency, SOC impact, and implementation effort.

current_trust_posture.current_posture: Target workstreams exist for routing logs, selector hash logging, no scoring-map leakage, trace integrity, failure autopsy, model change control, and evidence-retention mode.
current_trust_posture.roadmap_treatment: Turn HoloGov/HoloVerify targets into logs, procedures, tests, and change records.

created_artifacts.current_use: Baseline target architecture and data-flow scope.
created_artifacts.claim_boundary: Draft target/control architecture, not implementation proof.

created_artifacts.current_use: SOC 2 TSC mapping and gap register.
created_artifacts.claim_boundary: Gap assessment only, not readiness or auditor validation.

created_artifacts.current_use: Provider handling and verification workstream matrix.
created_artifacts.claim_boundary: Not provider contract review or zero-retention/DPA/BAA proof.
Evidence index only, not control operation proof.

created_artifacts.current_use: Approved-safe, conditional, and blocked buyer claims.
created_artifacts.claim_boundary: Draft control register requiring review gates.
Not public marketing copy or compliance claim.

created_artifacts.current_use: Starter Q&A for buyer security review.
created_artifacts.claim_boundary: Draft responses with status labels and missing evidence.

30_day_priorities.action: Create evidence folders, naming convention, owner roster, evidence tracker, and review cadence.
30_day_priorities.output: Trust evidence binder v0.2 with owners and due dates.

30_day_priorities.action: Freeze buyer-safe language and require review for questionnaire responses.
30_day_priorities.output: Claim review SOP and approved response pack.
30_day_priorities.dependency: Buyer claim register and questionnaire starter pack.

30_day_priorities.action: Create deployment-scope worksheet for HoloEngine, HoloGov, HoloVerify, HoloChat, HoloBuild, and optional HoloBrain.
Assign named owners to each SOC 2 gap and binder item.
30_day_priorities.dependency: SOC 2 gap assessment and binder index.

30_day_priorities.action: Draft identity/auth, RBAC, tenant isolation, admin/support access, and service-account design.
Convert metadata-only audit-log target into schema, excluded-field tests, and sample log requirements.
30_day_priorities.output: Audit-log schema and redaction test plan.

30_day_priorities.action: Build provider review checklist for OpenAI, xAI, and MiniMax covering terms, DPA, subprocessor, retention, zero retention, BAA/HIPAA, residency, and route/account/config.
Write IR policy, severity model, customer notification draft, evidence preservation steps, and tabletop plan.
Draft secure SDLC policy covering code review, branch protection, secret scanning, dependency scanning, release approval, and emergency changes.
30_day_priorities.output: Secure SDLC and change-control policy v0.1.

30_day_priorities.action: Write HoloVerify benchmark/evidence separation SOP covering storage, access, retention, scoring maps, public/internal boundary, and production separation.

60_day_priorities.action: Implement or document auth/RBAC/tenant-binding controls and run negative tests where deployment exists.
Collect deployed TLS/provider API transport, storage encryption, evidence-store encryption, backup encryption, and key-management proof.
Produce sample metadata-only audit logs and tests proving excluded fields are not logged by default.
Create secrets inventory, storage/rotation policy, CI/CD handling notes, and secret-scanning evidence.
Start dependency/SAST scanning, severity SLAs, exception register, remediation tracking, and pen-test planning.
Draft and test retention schedule, deletion SOP, customer request workflow, backup deletion handling, and evidence-retention exceptions.
Define support access approval, just-in-time access, customer authorization, logging, redaction, and break-glass review.
60_day_priorities.owner: Support Lead / Security Lead / Privacy Lead

60_day_priorities.action: Collect and review provider terms, DPAs, subprocessors, retention settings, and route/account/config evidence.
60_day_priorities.output: Provider contract and configuration evidence binder.

60_day_priorities.action: Create selector version/hash release manifest, route inventory, model/provider change procedure, and sample route-log requirements.
60_day_priorities.dependency: Logging design and model route inventory.

60_day_priorities.action: Run first IR tabletop including AI/model failure scenario and provider-route incident scenario.

90_day_priorities.action: Review each control for design, implementation, evidence, owner, and residual gap status.
Prepare candidate SOC 2 system description, TSC scope, carved-out services, subservice organizations, and evidence inventory.
Decide whether to pursue SOC 2 Type I, defer, or narrow scope.
90_day_priorities.output: SOC 2 Type I go/no-go decision record.

90_day_priorities.action: If Type I path is credible, define Type II observation-period prerequisites and operating cadence.
90_day_priorities.dependency: Stable controls and Type I decision.

90_day_priorities.action: Update design-partner packet, questionnaire pack, and claim register with verified evidence only.
Decide whether DPA/customer terms, privacy notice, subprocessor list, and deletion commitments are ready for counsel review.
Decide whether ISO 27001 or ISO 42001 mapping is useful now or should wait until SOC 2 foundation stabilizes.
Decide whether healthcare buyers are in scope; if yes, start BAA/provider and HIPAA legal analysis.
Decide whether any provider route can support a verified zero-retention claim.
90_day_priorities.owner: Legal / Security Lead / AI Governance Lead

90_day_priorities.action: Choose which external work is worth funding: SOC 2 readiness consultant, auditor scoping call, penetration test, privacy counsel, or provider contract review.
90_day_priorities.dependency: Control readiness review and buyer pressure.

Access, IR, vulnerability, secrets, and control owner matrix.
Data map, deletion SOP, retention schedule.
Deployment scope worksheet and customer-facing boundaries.
Selector hash, route logs, model change control.
Benchmark/evidence separation and scoring-map leakage tests.
Support access SOP and access-log requirements.

dependency_map.external_need: None unless production environment is unavailable.

evidence_artifacts_to_create.purpose: Central owner/status tracker with evidence links and due dates.
Assign named owners to SOC 2 and AI controls.
Define HoloEngine, HoloGov, HoloVerify, HoloChat, HoloBuild, and HoloBrain scope for each buyer/deployment.
Define auth, RBAC, tenant isolation, support access, service accounts, and reviews.
Define fields, excluded fields, redaction tests, and evidence samples.
Track OpenAI, xAI, MiniMax terms, DPA, BAA/HIPAA, subprocessors, retention, zero retention, residency, route/account/config.
Create severity model, escalation, customer notification, evidence preservation, tabletop plan.
Define code review, branch protection, scans, release approval, model/provider route change, emergency changes.
Define scans, severity SLAs, remediation, exceptions, pen-test plan.
Define retention schedule, deletion workflow, backup handling, evidence-retention exceptions.
Define approval, just-in-time access, customer authorization, logging, redaction, break-glass.
Route inventory, selector hash logging, model change control, failure autopsy, route-log samples.
Storage/access/retention separation, scoring-map controls, public/internal evidence boundary.
Backup policy, restore test, RTO/RPO, dependency map, DR runbook.
Candidate system description, TSC scope, control matrix, evidence inventory, carve-outs.

buyer_readiness_milestones.what_it_enables: Design-partner trust discussion using careful draft language.
buyer_readiness_milestones.claim_boundary: Can say trust-readiness buildout; cannot say ready/certified.

buyer_readiness_milestones.what_it_enables: More complete security questionnaire responses with owners and missing evidence visible.
buyer_readiness_milestones.required_evidence: Control owner matrix, provider checklist, IR/SDLC drafts.
buyer_readiness_milestones.claim_boundary: Answers remain safe_draft or conditional_tbd unless proof exists.

buyer_readiness_milestones.what_it_enables: Stronger buyer answers for selected controls that have samples/tests.
buyer_readiness_milestones.claim_boundary: Only claim the specific verified control and scope.

buyer_readiness_milestones.what_it_enables: Decide whether to pay for auditor or readiness consultant.
buyer_readiness_milestones.required_evidence: Control readiness review, system scope, evidence inventory, budget.
buyer_readiness_milestones.claim_boundary: Internal decision only, not readiness claim.

buyer_readiness_milestones.what_it_enables: Counsel-reviewed design-partner packet for selected buyers.
buyer_readiness_milestones.required_evidence: Legal review, claim register v0.2, verified evidence links.
buyer_readiness_milestones.claim_boundary: Still not certification or operating-effectiveness proof.

can_be_said_now: HoloEngine has repo-backed draft trust artifacts for enterprise diligence.
HoloEngine is the primary taxonomy; HoloGov is part of HoloEngine.
HoloVerify benchmark/evidence mode is separate from production customer mode in the trust architecture and claim language.
HoloChat and HoloBuild are connected surfaces only when included in a customer deployment.
The target production design uses bounded ALLOW / ESCALATE review and metadata-only audit logging by default.
Provider/vendor handling for OpenAI, xAI, and MiniMax is a verification workstream.
SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria areas and gaps have been identified.

unlocks_later.future_claim: Metadata-only audit logs are implemented for a deployment.
unlocks_later.unlock_condition: Runtime samples, redaction tests, log access/retention evidence.
Design, implementation proof, negative tests, access reviews.

unlocks_later.future_claim: Encryption in transit/at rest is verified.
unlocks_later.unlock_condition: TLS/provider API/internal transport evidence plus storage/backup/key-management evidence.

unlocks_later.future_claim: Provider DPA coverage exists for a provider route.
unlocks_later.unlock_condition: Signed/accepted DPA or contract evidence mapped to account/route/config.

unlocks_later.future_claim: Provider zero-retention handling exists for a provider route.
unlocks_later.unlock_condition: Provider-specific contract/config proof mapped to HoloEngine route and approved by Legal/Security.
SOP, implementation config, request workflow, backup handling, test evidence.
Route inventory, change policy, approvals, eval gates, rollback and customer-notice criteria.

remains_blocked: SOC 2 readiness, certification, compliance, Type I completion, Type II completion, or auditor validation.
Provider zero retention without provider-specific route/account/config evidence.
RAM-only processing, 0 ms retention, or memory zeroing.
Claims that customer payloads are never sent to providers.
Claims that HoloVerify benchmark/evidence retention is equivalent to production customer handling.
Claims that HoloChat or HoloBuild are automatically in HoloEngine trust scope.

decision_points.earliest_useful_timing: Around day 90, only after scope and control design evidence are stable.
decision_points.practical_recommendation: Do not buy a Type I audit until missing controls have owners and enough implementation evidence to avoid a weak report.

decision_points.earliest_useful_timing: After Type I readiness and a stable operating cadence.
decision_points.practical_recommendation: Treat as Phase 3; do not start the clock before controls are actually operating.

decision_points.earliest_useful_timing: After core security program artifacts exist.
decision_points.practical_recommendation: Defer unless a buyer requires ISO or SOC 2 scope work naturally produces most inputs.

decision_points.earliest_useful_timing: After AI governance controls are real enough to manage.
decision_points.practical_recommendation: Defer until HoloGov/HoloVerify controls have evidence, not just target design.

decision_points.earliest_useful_timing: Only if healthcare buyers are a near-term sales target.
decision_points.practical_recommendation: Do not pursue by default; run a counsel-led go/no-go if healthcare becomes strategic.

decision_points.earliest_useful_timing: After provider contracts and route/account/config mapping are available.
decision_points.practical_recommendation: Treat as provider-specific and route-specific; never generalize across all providers.

lean_startup_sequence: Spend internal time first on scope, owner matrix, evidence binder, claim controls, and policies.

Build only the evidence most likely to appear in buyer questionnaires: access control, tenant isolation, encryption, logging, provider handling, deletion, IR, SDLC, vulnerability management, support access.
Use a small external legal/provider review before promising DPA/BAA, zero retention, residency, HIPAA, or deletion terms.
Delay formal SOC 2 auditor spend until the control owner matrix and evidence binder show enough substance to support a useful Type I scope.
Prefer one tightly scoped SOC 2 Type I candidate system over a broad system that drags HoloChat, HoloBuild, HoloBrain, and HoloVerify into scope before they are ready.
Keep HoloVerify benchmark/evidence artifacts useful for AI assurance, but do not let them become production privacy claims.
Refresh buyer materials only after evidence changes; do not let roadmap language become sales claims.

requires_counsel: Support access terms if customer data may be accessed
requires_auditor_or_external_assessor: Formal SOC 2 Type I or Type II audit
requires_vendor_contracts_or_provider_re: BAA availability if healthcare is pursued

practical_next_actions: Appoint interim named owners for Trust, Legal, Security, Privacy, Engineering, Product, SRE, HoloGov, HoloVerify, and Support.
Create the trust binder v0.2 using the existing binder index and add due dates for every missing, TBD, and target_control_only item.
Convert the security questionnaire starter pack into a tracked response library that cannot be edited without claim-register review.
Prioritize one narrow deployment scope for HoloEngine/HoloGov before adding HoloChat or HoloBuild to formal trust scope.
Start provider review for OpenAI, xAI, and MiniMax with no zero-retention, DPA, BAA, HIPAA, or residency claims until proof exists.
Produce the first implementation evidence packets for access control, logging, encryption, SDLC, incident response, and HoloVerify separation.
At day 90, decide whether the evidence is strong enough to fund SOC 2 Type I scoping or whether another internal gap-closure cycle is the better use of budget.
